record.field: PHOTOREALISM MANDATE — This must look like an unretouched RAW photograph taken with a Hasselblad X2D. FACE: The model's face must look like a REAL human being, not a 3D render or AI composite. Render visible pores on nose/cheeks/forehead, subtle undereye texture, natural lip dryness lines, real skin translucency with visible blood vessels under thin-skin areas. Slight facial asymmetry is MANDATORY. EYES: Wet corneal reflection, visible iris fibres, natural eyelid crease depth, realistic tear film. HAIR: Individually resolved strands with natural flyaways. SKIN: Subsurface scattering, vellus hair on cheeks and jawline. NO porcelain/waxy/airbrushed/plastic skin. FABRIC: Physically plausible weave, stitching, seams, gravity-driven drape.

Environment: Pure, expansive, high-end minimalist fashion studio set. Bright, airy atmosphere with infinite white space. No visible wall seams, no horizontal floor lines, no architectural slices, no room corners. Clean floor-to-background transition with soft, natural bounce light. Background lock: seamless white studio cyclorama (#FFFFFF) only. STUDIO EQUIPMENT BAN: No visible studio equipment whatsoever — no softboxes, no flash heads, no light stands, no umbrellas, no reflectors, no C-stands. ONLY the model and the garment should exist in the frame.

[ANATOMY LOCK] CRITICAL: Ensure exactly two arms, two legs, and one head. Do NOT generate extra limbs, extra fingers, extra toes, or ghosting effects. Hands must have exactly five fingers each. No double heads or double necks. No warped human proportions or detached limbs. Any anatomical hallucination is a failure. [POSTURE STABILITY LOCK] Maintain physically plausible human weight distribution and bone structure. Joints must be naturally articulated. No impossible leg or arm angles.

GARMENT CONSTRUCTION IMMUTABLE LOCK: Every garment element must match the reference product images exactly. Collar type/shape, pocket count/type/placement, button count/spacing/style, zipper position, neckline cut, cuff style, sleeve length. Waistband type, leg opening, pleat structure, hem finish. Do NOT redesign, restyle, simplify, or reinterpret any garment piece. Do NOT hallucinate embellishments: no adding fringes, tassels, ruffles, patches that are not visible in the reference images.

COLOR FIDELITY LOCK: The generated garment color MUST match the reference images EXACTLY. Do not shift hue, saturation, or brightness. Treat the source images as the definitive color truth. Match the exact color hex from analysis. Any color deviation is a critical failure. COLOR DEPTH: Match not just the hue but also the SATURATION and BRIGHTNESS. A washed-out/faded color MUST remain washed-out/faded. A deep/vivid color MUST remain deep/vivid.

FABRIC TEXTURE FIDELITY LOCK: The generated fabric surface MUST match the reference images EXACTLY. Crinkle/crepe texture must NOT become corduroy. Smooth woven fabric must NOT become knitted. Ribbed knit must NOT become cable knit. The fabric's visual weight, drape behavior, and surface irregularity must match the reference. Any fabric texture substitution is a CRITICAL FAILURE.

BUTTON and HARDWARE FIDELITY LOCK: Every button, zipper, snap, hook, and rivet MUST match the reference images EXACTLY. Button size, shape, material, finish, color, opacity, and hole count MUST be replicated precisely. Small semi-translucent buttons must NOT become large opaque contrasting buttons. Button count, spacing, and alignment MUST match exactly.

ANTI-HALLUCINATION LOCK: Do NOT invent or add any decorative element not visible in the reference product images. Banned hallucinations: fringes, tassels, ruffles, visible contrasting embroidery when original has tone-on-tone texture, patches, beading, ribbon trim, lace inserts, extra zippers, chains. The garment must look EXACTLY like the reference, not a creative interpretation of it. [NECKLINE LOCK] Ensure neckline stays true to garment input. [EDGE/HEM LOCK] Maintain continuous, unbroken hem details. [HARDWARE LOCK] Do NOT invent fake chest pockets.

GARMENT IDEALIZATION LOCK: The generated garment must look FRESHLY STEAMED and PRISTINE — as if it just came off a professional steamer in a luxury boutique. REMOVE ALL: stains, dirt marks, dust, lint, pet hair, loose threads, storage wrinkles, fold creases, packaging lines, hanger marks, ironing burns, water spots, makeup transfer marks. The fabric surface must be SMOOTH and CLEAN at the macro level. Think: the difference between a crinkle-textured shirt fresh from a steamer vs one pulled from a suitcase.

LOCKED FIDELITY MODE: Preserve model identity and garment construction with strict consistency. The garment must be a 100% literal copy of the reference garment. Keep body proportions, limb lengths, shoulder/hip balance, and silhouette natural. Hardware MUST match exactly: count the buttons, match zipper styles, preserve rivets/snaps. Keep garment geometry unchanged with precise fit, drape, seam placement, neckline, waistline, dart map, closure positions, slit position, and hemline level. CROSS-VIEW CONSISTENCY: The EXACT SAME garment must appear in ALL views. Same color, pattern, buttons, collar, hemline, sleeve length, fabric texture.

FULL OUTFIT MANDATORY LOCK: The model MUST be fully dressed in ALL views including detail view. No bare midriff, no bare chest, no bare legs unless garment is a skirt/dress above knee. If the reference is an UPPER garment: model MUST also wear bottoms and shoes. If the reference is a LOWER garment: model MUST also wear a complete top and shoes. If the reference is a DRESS: model MUST wear shoes, minimal accessories. ANY nudity or missing garment is a CRITICAL FAILURE.

NEGATIVE: extra arms, extra legs, extra fingers, duplicated face, double head, ghosting limbs, warped anatomy, deformed body, mannequin, doll, plastic/waxy/porcelain skin, CGI look, beauty filter, airbrushed, over-smooth, blurry, low quality, collage, split screen, multi-panel, grid layout, montage, interior room, furniture, flat-lay, hanger, product-only shot, softbox, light stand, flash head, studio equipment, altered garment design, changed collar, missing/added pockets, changed hemline, warped seams, missing/extra buttons, distorted neckline, front-print-on-back, logo hallucination, invented embroidery, bare midriff, bare chest, nude model, wrong garment color, wrong fabric texture, wrong button style.

Zara-style editorial high-fashion aesthetic. Effortless, slightly asymmetrical model posing, dynamic posture, high-energy but calm demeanor. High-key airy studio lighting, soft natural glow, cinematic depth. Bright minimalist set with no visible seams or artifacts. Professional high-end catalog look. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP, hyper-realistic skin texture on pure solid white background (#FFFFFF). GARMENT FIDELITY OVERRIDE: This style directive affects ONLY model pose, camera angle, lighting mood, and background atmosphere. It must NEVER alter the garment's color, fabric texture, button hardware, or construction details.

Back-view integrity lock: if the back reference does NOT show logo/graphic/print/embroidery text, keep the back plain. Never mirror or copy front-only artwork onto the back panel. View integrity lock: front, side, back, and detail must remain physically truthful to references.

Detail fidelity lock: preserve buttons, labels/tags, stitching, seam lines, and neckline shape exactly. Do not move, remove, or invent buttons/labels. DETAIL VIEW CLOTHING REQUIREMENT: In the detail/macro view, the model MUST be wearing the FULL outfit. ALL closures must be FASTENED as designed. Show a close-up of garment construction detail while the model wears the complete outfit. No bare skin beyond wrists, neck, and face.
